'''Morgan Knowles''' (born 5 November 1996) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lk}} or {{rlp|sr}} for the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) in Australia.<ref name="St Helens sign up Morgan Knowles, Ricky Bailey, Jake Spedding and Levy Nzoungou">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12206/9956221/st-helens-sign-up-morgan-knowles-ricky-bailey-jake-spedding-and-levy-nzoungou|title=St Helens sign up Morgan Knowles, Ricky Bailey, Jake Spedding and Levy Nzoungou|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2015-08-19|accessdate=2016-09-10}}</ref> Knowles has played for both [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

==Background==
Knowles was born in [[Barrow-in-Furness]], Cumbria, England, and attended [[Ulverston Victoria High School]].<ref name="St Helens bow for Barrow Island junior Morgan Knowles">{{cite web|url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/sport/St-Helens-bow-for-Barrow-Island-junior-Morgan-Knowles-669651c7-bf24-41f9-9a73-20a731b3db81-ds|title=St Helens bow for Barrow Island junior Morgan Knowles|publisher=North West Evening Mail|date=2016-02-16|accessdate=2016-09-10}}</ref> He played junior rugby league for Barrow Island before signing for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at the age of 15.<ref name="Morgan Knowles and Matty Fleming sign new St Helens contracts">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37093217|title=Morgan Knowles and Matty Fleming sign new St Helens contracts|publisher=BBC|date=2016-08-16|accessdate=2016-09-10}}</ref>

==Career==
===St Helens===
Knowles made his first team debut for Saints in May 2015, appearing as a substitute in a [[Challenge Cup]] game against [[York City Knights]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Challenge Cup sixth round: St Helens 46-6 York |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/32711727 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=30 March 2023 |date=15 May 2015}}</ref> He established a regular place in the first team during the following year, and was rewarded with a new three-year contract at the club.<ref name="Morgan Knowles and Matty Fleming sign new St Helens contracts" />

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>
Knowles played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019 |last1=Unwin |first1=Will }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

Knowles played in St Helens [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 17 July 2021, he played for St. Helens in their 26-12 [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk}}</ref>
On 9 October 2021, he played in St. Helens [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
In the 2022 semi-final, Knowles was sent to the sin bin for a chicken wing tackle during St Helens 19-12 victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] which sent the club into their fourth consecutive grand final.  Knowles was later suspended for two matches.  St Helens appealed the decision which the RFL rejected.  Knowles and St Helens then put in a second appeal which was successful meaning he was free to play in the 2022 Super League Grand Final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12702892/st-helens-morgan-knowles-to-play-in-grand-final-after-winning-second-appeal-against-suspension/|title=St Helens' Morgan Knowles to play in Grand Final after winning second appeal against suspension|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Knowles played for St Helens in their [[2022 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
On 18 February 2023, Knowles played in St Helens 13-12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] in the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].<ref>{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 12 April 2023, Knowles was suspended for five matches and issued a £500 fine after he was placed on report for an illegal tackle during St Helens 14-6 loss to Wigan in round 8 of the competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23449194.st-helens-morgan-knowles-big-ban-tackle-mike-cooper/|title=St Helens' Morgan Knowles' big ban for tackle on Mike Cooper|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=11 April 2023 }}</ref>
On 22 May 2023, Knowles was suspended for two games following being red carded during the clubs Challenge Cup victory over [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]]. Knowles was sent off in the final minute of the game for a high tackle on Halifax hooker Tom Inman.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/morgan-knowles-receives-suspension-following-challenge-cup-red-card/|title=Morgan Knowles receives suspension following Challenge Cup red card|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=22 May 2023 }}</ref>
Knowles played 21 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table. Knowles played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which stopped them reaching a fifth successive grand final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=14 October 2023 }}</ref>
Knowles played 16 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. Knowles played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
'Soul destroying': Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|newspaper=The Observer|date=
28 September 2024|last1=
Bower|first1=
Aaron}}</ref>

===Dolphins (NRL)===
On 28 February 2025, it was announced that Knowles would be leaving St Helens at the end of the 2025 season, to join the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Knowles to leave St Helens for NRL at end of 2025|url =https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/cj6776zjrewo|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=28 February 2025}}</ref>
Knowles played 28 games for St Helens in his final year at the club as they reached the semi-finals before losing to Hull Kingston Rovers. Knowles was also the only St Helens player to be selected in Super League's dream team of 2025.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c04q6rqyrv6o/|title=Retiring Waerea-Hargreaves set to miss Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Representative===
On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he made a try scoring début for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in their 24-26 defeat to the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England’s [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=25 Jun 2021|accessdate=26 Jun 2021|title=England 24-26 Combined All Stars - Shaun Wane loses first game|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57618533}}</ref>
